title: Deployments, ReplicaSets, Rollouts, Strategies, Conditions, and Recovery · Cluster Case: K8s Image Config Rollout Identity
summary: A release changes image and config while progress stalls, old ReplicaSets remain, and a rollback would restore code without restoring external state. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s image config rollout identity increment in observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bind Pod template changes to immutable image digests, configuration identity, pull policy, provenance, and selected platform evidence.
Calculate RollingUpdate maxSurge, maxUnavailable, total capacity, readiness, termination, and rollout duration under a changed replica count.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.

version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Deployment rolls out image tag latest, stalls at 2 unavailable replicas, exceeds progress deadline, and has a bad ConfigMap change outside revision history. Bind immutable identity, strategy, conditions, pause, rollback, and verification. Competency probe: Bind Pod template changes to immutable image digests, configuration identity, pull policy, provenance, and selected platform evidence. A defensible solution applies K8s Image Config Rollout Identity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-image-config-rollout-identity — Bind Pod template changes to immutable image digests, configuration identity, pull policy, provenance, and selected platform evidence.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-deployments-rollouts-recovery-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-deployments-rollouts-recovery-5
    artifact: deployment.yaml
    decision: worked k8s deployments rollouts recovery 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      replicas: 4
      strategy: {maxSurge: 1, maxUnavailable: 0}
      image: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
    verify: kubectl rollout status deployment/status-api && kubectl get rs,pods -l app=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject mutable release identity, readiness-free rollout success, capacity arithmetic errors, or rollback presented as external-state recovery.
    repair: Bind image and config identity, calculate surge and unavailable capacity, inspect conditions and ReplicaSets, and coordinate roll forward or rollback.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

# Evidence: k8s-image-config-rollout-identity-dvj47m
  - competency: k8s-image-config-rollout-identity
    artifact: deployment.yaml
    decision: image config rollout identity
    environment: kubeadm recovery cluster restored onto replacement nodes
    fault-injection: withdraw the selected image digest after one node pulls it

    change: |
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      replicas: 4
      strategy: {maxSurge: 1, maxUnavailable: 0}
      image: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
    verify: kubectl rollout status deployment/status-api && kubectl get rs,pods -l app=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject mutable release identity, readiness-free rollout success, capacity arithmetic errors, or rollback presented as external-state recovery.
